description_long: This is a compact submachine gun with a minimalist, utilitarian design, featuring a straight, tubular receiver and a prominent detachable box magazine inserted vertically below the action. The rifle has a thin, folding metal stock at the rear, which is skeletal in structure, and a simple pistol grip for handling. The barrel is slim and extends forward from the receiver, ending in a plain muzzle with no visible attachments. The iron sights are basic, with a front post and a rear aperture integrated into the receiver and barrel assembly. The overall finish is matte, with a predominantly metallic appearance and some darker elements on the magazine. This weapon is reminiscent of mid-20th-century military submachine guns, designed for reliability and ease of use in urban environments.
description: A compact, metallic submachine gun with a folding skeletal stock, detachable box magazine, and simple iron sights. The design is utilitarian and matte, with a slim barrel and pistol grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, folding stock, pistol grip, and vertical magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the slim barrel, front sight, and narrow profile of the receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the folding stock, magazine, and overall symmetry of the rifle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the skeletal stock, rear sight, and the alignment of the receiver.
